close_reason: Added shared_client() with static OnceLock for connection pool reuse across instances
description:
src/lib.rs:97 — every DopplerBackend instance constructs its own reqwest::Client. The client owns a TLS configuration, a connection pool, and a DNS resolver. For a single-secret consumer this is fine; for a process that constructs many DopplerBackend instances (e.g. a daemon that creates one per secret URI on demand) it duplicates pools and hot connections that won't be reused across instances.

Doppler's API is rate-limited (per Doppler's docs, the limits are not generous for high-frequency callers). A daemon repeatedly handshake-ing TLS to api.doppler.com is wasteful.

Fix scope: this is a workspace-wide pattern question, not a doppler-specific one. Sibling crates (vault, scaleway-sm, bitwarden, barbican) likely share the same shape. Either (a) document explicitly that callers should share an Arc<DopplerBackend> across the URIs they want to access, or (b) introduce a workspace-level shared client (e.g. a once-cell reqwest::Client that secretx-core or each backend uses by default, with an opt-in override).

Flag here for visibility — full fix is workspace-wide and out of single-crate scope.
title: Each from_uri allocates a new reqwest::Client; no connection pool reuse
